features.name: Second Expertise
features.description: You gain additional expertise in one skill of your choice.

features.name: Second Chances
features.description: When you see an attacker with a proficiency in one of your skills and they hit you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage. You may also double any ability check that uses one of your chosen proficiencies.

features.name: Second Wind
features.description: You can use a bonus action on your turn to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your Rogue level

features.name: Sneak Attack (1st Level)
features.description: Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one target you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll, or if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it (no line of sight required). You don't need advantage if another enemy of the target has incapacitated the target

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When a hostile creature's attack hits you and you take no damage on the hit, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's remaining damage

features.name: Evasion
features.description: If you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed, and only half damage if you fail

features.name: Expertise (1st Choice)
features.description: Choose one skill. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses that skill

features.name: Sneak Attack (2nd Level)
features.description: You can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one target you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll, or if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it (no line of sight required). You don't need advantage if another enemy of the target has incapacitated the target

features.name: Elude
features.description: When a hostile creature that you can see attacks you and has advantage on the attack roll, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. The attacker must make the attack roll without benefiting from any situational bonuses

features.name: Deceitful
features.description: You have advantage on Disguise checks and on Charisma (Deception) checks to feign injury or madness. You can suppress one of your abilities scores while making such a check, treating it as 12 instead

features.name: Evasion (Revised)
features.description: If you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed, and only half damage if you fail. This feature changes at 20th level

features.name: Sneaky Escape
features.description: When you roll initiative and have advantage on that roll, you can spend your action and the unused portion of your movement to become invisible until the start of your next turn, or until you take a melee attack. You also gain temporary hit points equal to 1d10 + your level. This feature changes at 20th level

features.name: Sneaky Escape (Revised)
features.description: When you roll initiative and have advantage on that roll, you can spend your action and the unused portion of your movement to become invisible until the start of your next turn, or until you take a melee attack. You also gain temporary hit points equal to 1d10 + your level. This feature changes at 20th level

features.name: Stealth Expertise
features.description: You have proficiency in the Dexterity (Stealth) skill.

features.name: Thief of the Nine Fingers
features.description: You gain expertise in one skill of your choice.

features.name: Pickpocket’s Luck
features.description: You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws to avoid opportunity attacks while you are unseen by a creature.

features.name: Master of Disguise
features.description: You can use the Disguise Kit to create a disguise, and you can add your proficiency bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks you make with that kit.

features.name: Thief’s Cant
features.description: You know the Thieves' Cant language. As an action, you can cast Detect Thoughts on yourself (no concentration required).

features.name: Master of Many Forms
features.description: You gain the ability to use your Action Surge feature twice before completing a long rest.
At level 1, choose an archetype that emulates an aspect of a 3rd-level thief: Assassin or Trickster. Your archetype choice grants features at 1st level, then again at 13th and 17th levels.

features.name: Feature
features.description: You can react to someone making an attack roll against you by imposing disadvantage on that attack if you take the Dodge action on your previous turn.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: If you are subjected to an attack that allows saving throws to take half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw.

features.name: Spells Known
features.description: You know the cure wounds spell. At higher levels, you learn other spells as well.

features.name: Domain Feature
features.description: Choose a domain. This grants you features and a domain spell at 2nd level and later.
